skill.name: keyword-velocity-tracker
skill.description: Calculate literature growth velocity and acceleration to assess research field trends.
skill.summary: Analyze publication growth rates and acceleration for any research keyword to determine field development stage.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate research analysis tool that calculates publication growth velocity and acceleration. All 77 static findings are false positives: weak cryptographic algorithm detections match on non-cryptographic terms like 'algorithm description'; system reconnaissance findings match generic data processing patterns; shell backtick findings are markdown code blocks in documentation; certificate/key file findings match variable names containing 'key'; and path traversal findings match security checklist documentation. The prior audit result JSON confirms this skill passed all security veto gates with a PASS status. The only genuine behavior is reading user-provided JSON data and writing analysis results to a user-specified output path.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detected in Non-Cryptographic Context
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 7 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across SKILL.md, scripts/main.py, and audit result JSON. All matches are on non-cryptographic text such as argparse descriptions, YAML frontmatter, JSON output fields, and markdown documentation. The skill performs no cryptographic operations.

high_findings.title: Certificate or Key File False Match on Variable Name
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'certificate/key files' at scripts/main.py:516 which is passing keyword argument to the analyze method, and at SKILL.md:163 which is a Python import statement in documentation. No actual certificate, key file, or credential handling exists.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence False Match on Security Checklist
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged SKILL.md lines 220 and 223 for 'path traversal sequence'. These lines are part of a security checklist documenting that path traversal is prevented, not performing traversal. The actual file read at scripts/main.py:505 uses argparse for safe path handling.

high_findings.title: Heuristic Suspicious Combination Based on False Positives
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged a SUSPICIOUS COMBINATION of Filesystem + Credentials + Network. This heuristic finding is built entirely on false positives from the other high findings listed above. The skill has no credential handling and the sole URL is a source attribution link in documentation.

medium_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution in Markdown Documentation Only
medium_findings.description: Scanner found 44 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md. All instances are markdown code blocks using backticks for formatting example commands (python scripts/main.py, python -m py_compile). These are documentation examples, not executable code.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operation
medium_findings.description: Python file write operation at scripts/main.py:525 writes analysis results to a user-specified output path via argparse --output flag. This is legitimate CLI tool behavior. The script reads input from --data-file and writes results to --output, both provided by the user.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Match on Data Processing Code
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 11 instances of 'system reconnaissance' across main.py, SKILL.md, and audit result JSON. The matches in main.py (lines 127-141) are within the exponential smoothing method. SKILL.md matches (lines 53, 65, 247) are documentation about output discipline and test cases. No system reconnaissance behavior exists.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Source Attribution Link
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged a hardcoded URL at SKILL.md line 7. This URL is a source attribution link to the GitHub repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). It is documentation metadata, not a network call or data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled File Read Path
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/main.py reads a JSON data file from a user-supplied path via argparse --data-file flag. The path is provided at runtime and used directly in open(). While this is standard CLI behavior, input validation is limited to argparse basic handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled File Write Path
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/main.py writes JSON output to a user-supplied path via argparse --output flag. The file path is used directly in open() with write mode. This is standard CLI tool behavior but could overwrite existing files.

content.user_title: Track research keyword velocity and field development stage
content.value_statement: Researchers cannot easily determine if a field is growing, maturing, or declining. This skill calculates publication growth velocity and acceleration from time series data to classify field development stage with confidence scores.
actual_capabilities: Calculate year-over-year publication growth rate for any research keyword
Determine field development stage as embryonic, growth, mature, or decline
Generate multi-year trend predictions with decreasing confidence levels
Analyze growth acceleration to detect field momentum changes
Produce structured JSON output with velocity series and insights
limitations: Requires pre-collected time series publication count data as input
Prediction confidence decreases significantly beyond 3 years
Does not fetch or search for publication data from external sources
Requires at least 2 years of data; 5 or more years recommended for reliable analysis

use_cases.title: Identify emerging research fields
use_cases.description: A research strategist analyzes publication growth rates across multiple keywords to identify fast-growing fields that may warrant new research programs or funding.

use_cases.title: Assess field maturity for grant prioritization
use_cases.description: A grant reviewer evaluates acceleration trends to distinguish growing fields (high acceleration) from mature or declining fields before allocating funding.

use_cases.title: Track discipline trends over time
use_cases.description: An academic librarian monitors publication velocity for key disciplines annually to build trend reports and guide collection development decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword velocity analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the growth trend for the keyword 'artificial intelligence ethics' using this publication data: [{"year": 2018, "count": 120}, {"year": 2019, "count": 180}, {"year": 2020, "count": 310}, {"year": 2021, "count": 520}, {"year": 2022, "count": 780}, {"year": 2023, "count": 950}]. Tell me the current velocity, acceleration, and field stage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick assessment of a single keyword with default parameters

prompt_templates.title: Custom time window for sensitivity analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run keyword velocity analysis for 'machine learning in healthcare' with data: [{"year": 2019, "count": 400}, {"year": 2020, "count": 550}, {"year": 2021, "count": 720}, {"year": 2022, "count": 890}, {"year": 2023, "count": 1050}]. Use a time window of 2 years instead of the default 3 to see if the stage changes with a shorter window.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing analysis sensitivity to time window parameter

prompt_templates.title: Comparative keyword analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I have publication data for two research topics. Topic A: [{"year": 2018, "count": 50}, {"year": 2019, "count": 65}, {"year": 2020, "count": 90}, {"year": 2021, "count": 200}, {"year": 2022, "count": 450}, {"year": 2023, "count": 800}]. Topic B: [{"year": 2018, "count": 1000}, {"year": 2019, "count": 1100}, {"year": 2020, "count": 1150}, {"year": 2021, "count": 1200}, {"year": 2022, "count": 1180}, {"year": 2023, "count": 1220}]. Compare their velocity, acceleration, and stage. Which field is emerging and which is mature?
prompt_templates.scenario: Side-by-side comparison of two keywords to guide research investment decisions

prompt_templates.title: Trend prediction with future outlook
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 'quantum computing' publication data: [{"year": 2017, "count": 200}, {"year": 2018, "count": 260}, {"year": 2019, "count": 340}, {"year": 2020, "count": 480}, {"year": 2021, "count": 650}, {"year": 2022, "count": 920}, {"year": 2023, "count": 1300}]. Predict 5 years forward and tell me when growth might start slowing. Explain what drives your confidence level for each prediction year.
prompt_templates.scenario: Strategic planning with long-term trend prediction

output_examples.input: Analyze keyword 'artificial intelligence ethics' using data from 2018 to 2023.
output_examples.output: The keyword 'artificial intelligence ethics' shows a current velocity of 0.22 (22% growth per year) and acceleration of 0.04. The field is in the growth stage with 87% confidence. Growth is accelerating, indicating rising research interest. The trend direction is upward. Prediction for 2024 estimates 1,150 publications with 75% confidence.

output_examples.input: Compare 'machine learning' and 'quantum computing' growth trends.
output_examples.output: Machine learning shows a velocity of 0.15 with negative acceleration of -0.03, placing it in the mature stage with stable trend. Quantum computing shows a velocity of 0.41 with acceleration of 0.12, placing it in the growth stage with upward trend. Quantum computing research is expanding rapidly while machine learning publications have stabilized.

best_practices: Provide at least 5 years of data for reliable trend analysis and stage classification
Use consistent data sources and time ranges when comparing multiple keywords
Review the smoothing parameter when data has high year-to-year variance
anti_patterns: Using data from a single year or fewer than 2 time points for analysis
Comparing keywords across different time ranges without adjusting for baseline differences
Interpreting short-term fluctuations in growth rate as long-term field trends

faq.question: What data format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill expects a JSON array of objects with 'year' (integer) and 'count' (integer) fields, like [{"year": 2020, "count": 100}].

faq.question: How many years of data do I need?
faq.answer: At least 2 years are required. For reliable stage classification and trend analysis, 5 or more years are recommended.

faq.question: What do the development stages mean?
faq.answer: Embryonic means low base and slow growth. Growth means accelerating publications. Mature means stable or slowing growth. Decline means publication volume is decreasing.

faq.question: How accurate are the future predictions?
faq.answer: Predictions rely on historical trend extrapolation. Confidence starts at 0.90 for year 1 and decreases by 0.15 per year, with a minimum of 0.30. Longer predictions are less reliable.

faq.question: Can I compare multiple keywords with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You can run analysis on each keyword separately and compare their velocity, acceleration, and stage results. Ensure you use consistent time ranges for meaningful comparisons.

faq.question: Does this skill search for publication data automatically?
faq.answer: No. You must provide pre-collected time series publication count data. The skill does not connect to any external database or API to fetch publication records.
